log_entries.thought_preview: The sample overview confirms this is a PE executable (30KB) tagged as a 'spreader' with an overlay. It's packed with Mpress (MPRESS). I need to get all IOC types to understand the full scope of artifa

IOC summary shows 3 IPs, 2 domains, 2 dropped files, and 1 process created. No persistence keys found. I need to investigate the network activity in detail to see if these IPs/domains are malicious C2

Network activity shows connections to two Australian domains (backpackinglight.com.au and littlepaperlane.com.au) on port 443. These appear to be legitimate e-commerce sites, suggesting the malware ma

Two files were dropped: 'papers.exe' (root) and a copy in Temp with a different SHA256. The different hashes suggest the Temp file might be unpacked or modified. I need to check process activity to se

Process activity shows the malware spawns itself in Temp and loads various Windows system DLLs (shdocvw, ole32, rasapi32, etc.), which is typical for a downloader/spreader. No new mutexes were created

Registry changes show no persistence keys or other keys set, which contradicts the initial overview that mentioned HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Direct3D\MostRecentApplication\Name. This might be a false po

I have gathered all available data. The sample is a 30KB Mpress-packed executable with anti-VM strings. It connects to two Australian domains (backpackinglight.com.au and littlepaperlane.com.au) on po
